title: Progression blockers in Cyberpunk 2077

evidence.content:
My expectations were probably too high, but I find this game to be just a good demonstration of the REDengine.  Yup, it's a huge open world. Yes, it has stunning graphics and realism. 
But there isn't a real RPG game here, at least not in the sense I'm used to (especially playing Japanese stuff).

Great visually, and the city looks fantastic, but that's it for me, really. Mechanically tiring, boring, plot-wise too much talking and info-dumps. On the rails affair. And NPCs still lock in mid-air or in the floor. Finished Risen recently, and obviously that game had worse story and graphics, but

It's like CyberPunk themed Grand Theft Auto with far better graphics and more in-depth plot and play.

However, do you enjoy having to click through literal hours of dialogue and cut scenes, which you often can't skip, and have to sit there and click to progress..? I don't.

For that reason, and man

I'm sorry, but the only good thing in this game is the visuals, the aesthetics. The story is flat and uninteresting. There is nothing deep in this game. No groundbreaking gameplay mechanics. No amazing story. Yeah, they fixed the bugs, but it's, fundamentally, a mediocre game. A game afraid of darin

Very good game and fun but ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ it is short, after 20 hours of main story content i already hit the point of no return and in those 20 hours you spend like 1 of those hours with the cool guys tops
